Identification.LongTitle: An Act respecting the Marine Mammal Regulations (seal fishery observation licence)
Identification.ShortTitle: An Act respecting the Marine Mammal Regulations (seal fishery observation licence)
Identification.RunningHead: Marine Mammal Regulations (seal fishery observation licence)

Provision.Text: This enactment requires the Governor in Council to amend the Marine Mammal Regulations to increase the distance that a person must maintain from another person who is fishing for seals, except under the authority of a seal fishery observation licence.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: Within 60 days after this Act comes into force, the Governor in Council is to make the following amendments to the Marine Mammal Regulations:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 32(2)(d) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: whether the applicant has a stated aim of disrupting the seal fishery or has been convicted, in the five years preceding the application for the licence, of tagging, marking or moving a live seal, of contravening subsection 33(1) or of violating a condition of a seal fishery observation licence; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 33(1) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2), no person shall, except under the authority of a seal fishery observation licence issued by the Minister, approach within one nautical mile of a person who is fishing for seals.

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 33(2)(e) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: to a person who resides on land within one nautical mile of a person who is fishing for seals.
